data_coverage_summary: Poka publicly describes flexible integration tooling through REST APIs, Automate iPaaS workflows, Data Lake exports, and SCIM provisioning for connected-worker and digital manufacturing data exchange.
buyer_guidance: Use Poka integrations and APIs for connected-worker, training, forms, identity, and operational workflow integrations across manufacturing environments. This is not a telemetry platform, but it does support asset-oriented workflow data and writeback.
overview: Poka's public integrations page describes four public integration approaches: Poka Automate for iPaaS workflow automation, Poka API for RESTful third-party integrations, Poka Data Lake for analytics export, and Poka SCIM for user and user-group provisioning. The public use cases and customer stories show integrations for SAP-driven maintenance resolution, Tableau-backed safety reporting with forms, and Workday-connected personnel training data, while the site repeatedly positions Poka as a system for connecting people, data, processes, tasks, and systems in digital manufacturing. This is a write-capable connected-worker integration surface with supported asset-oriented workflows, but not a telemetry platform. Asset data is Supported. Telemetry data is Unsupported. Writeback is Supported.

available_data.description: Poka publicly describes integrations that synchronize workforce and operational data into its connected-worker platform.
data_points: Users and user groups via SCIM
Data-lake export to BI and analytics tools

available_data.category: Safety, forms, and asset-performance use cases
available_data.description: The public integration stories also show Poka being used for plant-floor workflow improvement and equipment-related operational support.
data_points: Digital manufacturing process and task integration

ingest_considerations: Poka's public evidence is capability- and use-case-oriented rather than endpoint-oriented, so schema-level integration work will still require partner or customer technical detail beyond the public site.

The strongest public signals are around workflow, user provisioning, and analytics exchange rather than raw device telemetry, so telemetry support should remain Unsupported until public evidence shows otherwise.
No exact public machine-readable spec artifact was confirmed for Poka.

source_evidence.evidence_notes: Poka's public integrations page describes RESTful APIs, an Automate iPaaS platform, Data Lake exports, and SCIM provisioning, and customer stories explicitly reference SAP maintenance-resolution workflows, Tableau-backed safety forms, and Workday-connected personnel training data.
